laneSizingPolicy.splitRequiredWhen: A routeTaskId exceeds the recommended maximum or mixes blocker/catalog/substrate work that should not share a write scope.

lanes.purpose: Finish the two remaining focused reducer-spine diagnostic drivers using existing HP lifecycle and concentration owners.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Death Saving Throw and Concentration teardown replay through reducer entrypoints and shared owners, with no driver-local HP/concentration ledger.

lanes.purpose: Route common weapon, spell attack, creature attack, and stat-block action procedures through the reducer subject/fill/result surface.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Route evidence shows attack and stat-block action branches consume shared reducer state, not fixture identity or adapter-local action ordering.

lanes.purpose: Route Command/scalar spell-effect frontier behavior through battle spell-effect owners and refresh the next-turn command option evidence.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Command and scalar buff routes reuse spell/action-effect substrate and do not create a local ordering island.

lanes.splitRecommendation: Split into 4 sublanes to keep every lane under the preferred 70-obligation cap: damage/stat-block, movement/reactions/shove, spell/ability, and feature profile.
lanes.purpose: Prove reusable rule-core components before later battle and character routes depend on them.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Component route evidence exists without turning rule-core witnesses into production battle-state owners.

lanes.purpose: Prove attack damage disposition, HP damage, and stat-block control component connectors before battle action routes depend on them.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Damage, HP, and stat-block control component evidence exists without turning rule-core witnesses into production battle-state owners.

lanes.purpose: Prove movement, reaction/continuation, and shove reusable component connectors before battle movement/effect routes depend on them.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Movement, reaction, and shove component evidence exists without creating battle reducer owners or fixture identity dispatch.

lanes.purpose: Prove ability/skill/command and spell procedure component connectors before battle spell/effect and character routes depend on them.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Ability/skill/command and spell component evidence exists as reusable rule-core facts, not battle adapter local replay state.

lanes.purpose: Prove feature profile semantics component connector before battle feature substrate and later level-promotion routes depend on it.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Feature profile component evidence exists without admitting authored feature identity dispatch into production runtime semantics.

lanes.purpose: Route level-1/2 character creation choices through character-owned creation state and support admission, not identity dispatch.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Character creation evidence records durable character-state ownership and keeps authored identity at selection/catalog boundaries.

lanes.purpose: Route level-1/2 sheet projections through sheet-owned state and reusable component facts.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Sheet evidence records source fields and derived projections without duplicate runtime state.

lanes.purpose: Route character-to-battle handoff and settlement through character/sheet/battle owners.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Battle handoff evidence consumes character and sheet substrates instead of restating them in battle adapters.

lanes.splitRecommendation: Split into six sublanes: one catalog-ready lane and five blocker/substrate lanes grouped by generic route vocabulary/write scope.
lanes.purpose: Resolve level-1 spell selected-identity substrate blockers and keep spell identity outside production dispatch.
Route catalog-ready selected spell witnesses through existing save-gated, repeat-save, companion, and roll-modifier substrates while keeping selected spell identity outside production dispatch.
dependencies: existing save-gated, repeat-save, companion lifecycle, touch delivery, and roll-modifier route connectors
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Accepted evidence routes selected catalog witnesses through existing generic substrates, records level-2/3 condition-save branches as out-of-scope, and does not add production selected spell identity dispatch.
lanes.blockerHandling: If a branch cannot be routed through existing connector evidence, record a concrete substrate blocker and do not count it as accepted.

lanes.purpose: Admit selected damage-spell witnesses only through generic spell attack, save-gated, chained/object-target, or active-effect route subjects.
dependencies: CP1 spell attack/save-gated reducer routes merged
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Damage-spell selected witnesses count only when behavior is admitted by generic route subjects, not selected spell names.
lanes.blockerHandling: Add generic QNT route subjects only when cleanroom QNT/RAW facts support them; otherwise record branch-level blockers and exclude those branches from accepted replay.

lanes.purpose: Route weapon-hosted and buff spell behavior through typed rider, scalar, held-weapon, mark-transfer, condition-immunity, and temporary-HP substrates.
dependencies: CP1/CP2 spell, attack, scalar buff, and feature-profile connectors merged
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Accepted branches route through generic rider/scalar/held-weapon substrates; mark-transfer and condition-immunity branches remain blocked until generic route subjects exist.
lanes.blockerHandling: Split accepted existing-substrate branches from unresolved active-effect branches; do not count mark-transfer or condition-immunity branches without generic QNT route subjects.

lanes.purpose: Admit protection, charm, and ward behavior through target-admission, active-effect, condition-lifecycle, and target-interdiction facts.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Accepted all 16 protection, charm, and ward rows through copied generic route connectors and reducer-entrypoint route replay; selected spell identity remains outside production dispatch.
lanes.blockerHandling: No FU01D selected rows remain blocked after the copied route connector refresh; future rows still require copied QNT/RAW-derived generic route subjects before acceptance.

lanes.purpose: Route armor-class spell effects through generic armor-class projection plus active-effect/reaction owners.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Armor-class spell effects route through generic AC projection and active-effect/reaction owners; the level-3 reaction branch remains out-of-scope.
lanes.blockerHandling: Do not model armor-class behavior by selected spell identity; add generic AC route subjects or record the AC substrate blocker.

lanes.purpose: Admit spatial spell witnesses through generic light, outline, area, hazard, movement, falling, forced-movement, and object-boundary route subjects.
dependencies: CP4 small movement/form substrate lane merged
CP1/CP2 reaction and movement component facts available
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Spatial spell witnesses count only through generic spatial/light/area/movement route subjects.
lanes.blockerHandling: Expected to produce blocker evidence; add generic route subjects only when supported by cleanroom QNT/RAW facts, otherwise leave branch-level blockers.

lanes.splitRecommendation: Split into three sublanes: governor/Quickened first, then save/range/target options and reroll/damage/projection options.
lanes.purpose: Route Sorcery Point, casting-time, same-turn spell, and metamagic option substrates without selected-identity dispatch.
Establish shared Sorcery Point spend, one-option-per-spell, selected-option admission, Bonus Action casting-time, and same-turn level-1-plus spell locks.
dependencies: Prior CP1 spell/action reducer substrate merged
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Quickened and generic metamagic governor evidence routes through reducer entrypoints, typed subject/fill variants, BattleResolutionResult, feature-resource/action-economy/spell-procedure owners, and typed option facts; selected option names stay at catalog/fixture/admission boundaries.
lanes.blockerHandling: If option admission, Sorcery Point spend, same-turn spell locks, or route-owner evidence cannot be expressed without authored identity dispatch, record branch-level blockers instead of inferring TypeScript behavior.

lanes.purpose: Route Careful, Heightened, Distant, and Twinned through saving throw, roll-mode, damage-adjustment, condition, object-boundary, target-selection, and feature-resource owners.
dependencies: CP4 small feature substrate batch merged
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Careful/Heightened/Distant/Twinned evidence routes through typed metamagic effect facts and reducer owners, without production dispatch on option names.
lanes.blockerHandling: Block only affected branches if target-list, target-choice, saving-throw, or object-boundary facts are missing from QNT/corpus input.

lanes.purpose: Route Empowered, Seeking, Subtle, Transmuted, and Extended through damage roll, attack roll, damage type, hit point, component, action-economy, active-effect, concentration, and feature-resource owners.
dependencies: CP1/CP2 spell, attack-roll, damage-roll, and hit-point component evidence merged
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Reroll/damage/projection metamagic evidence routes through typed option facts and reducer owners; resource spend/rejection, reroll fills, damage-type choice fills, and duration/component projections are reducer-shaped facts, not adapter-local replay state.
lanes.blockerHandling: Record branch-level blockers if damage dice, attack reroll, component projection, duration/concentration, or damage-type substitution facts are missing from cleanroom QNT/corpus.

lanes.splitRecommendation: Can run as two or three small lanes based on write scope.
lanes.purpose: Cover smaller feature/species/movement/form/weapon-mastery substrates after core component and character substrates exist.

lanes.splitRecommendation: Split into 4-6 lanes: riders, active effects, reactions, companion/object, ability/search, independent spell attack.
lanes.purpose: Finish remaining battle families after generic feature/catalog substrates exist.
Route passive trait, passive roll-mode, and feature-resource substrates without authored identity dispatch.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Evidence routes passive/resource/roll-mode behavior through typed feature and battle owners rather than selected species, feature, or spell identity.

lanes.purpose: Route zero-HP stabilization, active form lifecycle, and movement/forced-movement substrates through existing battle owners.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Evidence routes stabilization, form, and movement behavior through HP lifecycle, active-form, movement-resource, turn-boundary, and battle owners.

lanes.purpose: Route weapon mastery property, breath weapon replacement, and active feature spell-benefit substrates through battle reducer owners.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Evidence routes property/area-save/feature spell-benefit behavior by typed property and feature facts, not authored identity.

lanes.purpose: Route after-hit rider ownership and weapon-hosted rider procedures through generic rider/weapon-hosted substrates without authored identity dispatch.
Route active-effect lifecycle, repeat-save, scalar modifier, turn-boundary, and zero-HP mid-resolution behavior through durable active-effect owners.
Route interrupt-stack and reaction casting-time behavior through reaction/interrupt owners and public reducer entrypoints.
Route companion lifecycle and object-boundary spell behavior through generic companion/object substrates or record connector blockers.
Route ability-check/search choice behavior through typed ability/search substrates and reducer route evidence.
Route chained attack and independent spell-attack sequence behavior through sequence/procedure owners without spell identity dispatch.

Reduce CP6 target-side blockers by routing generic condition-immunity plus turn-start Temporary Hit Points and marked-damage transfer effects through shared BattleState reducer subjects, without authored spell identity dispatch.
dependencies: copied FU01C QNT/guidance present in cleanroom-input
lanes.acceptanceSummary: The four selected FU01C rows replay through observed reducer entrypoints and generic active-effect/marked-effect subjects. Evidence must replace target blockers with accepted route rows only when route events come from BattleEntrypointTrace, not adapter-local synthesis.

lanes.purpose: Reduce CP7 target-side blockers by bridging selected level-1 damage-spell witness rows to existing generic chained spell-attack sequence and object-target spell-attack route substrates, without selected spell identity dispatch.
dependencies: copied chained attack sequence and Starry Wisp object route connectors present in cleanroom-input
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Merged and verified. The two selected FU01B rows replay through observed reducer entrypoints by reusing generic chained spell-attack sequence and object-target spell-attack substrates; accepted coverage is route-shape evidence only, because selected damage-spell witness payloads remain projection-helper derived.

lanes.purpose: Reduce CP7 target-side blockers by routing ordinary/pre-hit weapon setup rows through existing generic weapon attack discovery, target-fill, and attack-roll route shapes, without widening rider-specific substrates or using authored identity.
dependencies: RR05 generic weapon attack route evidence present
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Merged and verified. The five selected rows replay through observed BattleEntrypointTrace.route_events using generic WeaponAttack route subjects/fills and independent expected qRoute witnesses. Scenario transition rows and MagicWeaponTargetItem rows remain blocked.

lanes.purpose: Reduce the two Magic Weapon item-target CP7 blockers using the copied WeaponEnhancementItemTargetRouteSubject route connector without adding MagicWeaponTargetItem to reducer-owned hole/fill vocabulary.
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Merged and verified. doDiscoverMagicWeapon and doFillMagicWeaponTarget replay through observed BattleEntrypointTrace.route_events and match the copied WeaponEnhancementItemTargetRouteSubject qRoute: start battle, discover with empty holes and BattleItemTargetBoundaryOwner, then resolve without fill with BattleActiveEffectOwner. MagicWeaponTargetItem remains outside reducer-owned hole/fill vocabulary.

lanes.purpose: Reduce CP7 target-side blockers by first identifying the generic route owners for Mage Armor target admission/rejection and duration expiry from existing cleanroom evidence, then accepting only rows that replay through production reducer entrypoints without selected spell identity dispatch.
dependencies: FU01D generic target-admission route evidence present for comparison
active-effect lifecycle route evidence present for comparison
lanes.acceptanceSummary: Merged and verified as a blocker/research result with zero accepted rows. The three Mage Armor admission/lifecycle rows remain target-blocked because the copied reducer-route inventory has no Mage Armor qRoute connector or generic armor-class target-admission/active-effect lifecycle route owner; accepting them in target code would be source/corpus inference, not cleanroom evidence.

lanes.purpose: Accept the three remaining Mage Armor admission/lifecycle rows by replaying them through the refreshed generic SpellBaseArmorClassEffectRouteSubject connector and observed production reducer entrypoints.
dependencies: generic spell base Armor Class route connector present
nine scenario transition rows excluded from the refreshed reducer-route denominator as transit-only fixture sequencing rows
acceptanceCriteria: Observed replay for each selected row goes through start_battle_observed, discover_battle_acts, resolve_battle_subject, or equivalent existing observed reducer entrypoints that emit BattleEntrypointTrace.route_events.
Observed route events match cleanroom-input/qnt/battle-runtime/battle-runtime-spell-base-armor-class-effect.route.mbt.qnt for target admission, armored target rejection, and duration expiry surfaces.
Production code does not dispatch on Mage Armor name/id/provenance; selected identity remains fixture/admission/catalog data only.
The nine scenario transition rows remain out-of-scope transit-only and are not accepted by target replay evidence.
lanes.blockerHandling: If the refreshed connector is still insufficient, record the exact missing generic owner/fill/subject fact instead of synthesizing a selected spell route.
lanes.completionNote: Merged and verified in CP8; refreshed campaign coverage is 659/659 in-scope obligations.

standardVerification: focused cargo tests for selected drivers
cargo test reducer_spine_contract_adapter_replays_all_branches when battle reducer substrate is touched
